LAW AND MOTION, DEFENDANT 490 POST STREET, LLC'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCES. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF MUST FILE A FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT TO EXPLAIN OR CLARIFY THE INCONSISTENCY REGARDING THE PURCHASER OF THE PROPERTY BETWEEN THIS COMPLAINT AND THE COMPLAINT IN THE OTHER PENDING CONSTRUCTION MATTER REGARDING THE SAME PROPERTY. PLAINTIFF MUST FILE ITS 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT WITHIN 10 DAYS OF NOTICE OF ENTRY OF THIS ORDER. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. (302)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jan-11-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. GORDON D. MCAULEY OF GUMBINER & ESKRIDGE LLP, TEL. NO. (925) 933-5800 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. RONA CARELLA OF THE CASSINAT LAW CORPORATION, TEL. NO.(916) 393-3030 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS 490 POST STREET LLC A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY, UNIVERSITY CAPITAL MANAGEMENT, INC., A CALIFORNIA and BROADMOOR CAPITAL CORPORATION's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ADJDICATION - There are triable issues of fact whether Defendants failed to disclose material facts during the sale, and summary judgment is denied. (See evidence cited by Plaintiffs in response to UMF 8, 17-19, 21, 25, 27, 29, 33, 34, 36-38, 42, 45, 47, 48, 50-52, 57-58.)
1. Group is a proper party to this action if defendants' misrepresentations/concealment induced it to occupy suite 1700. Defendants fail to shift the burden on this issue. Group was part of the negotiations for the purchase of the property and agreed to occupy the premises based on Defendants' alleged misrepresentations and concealments. Koonce was the negotiator for both Group and Properties (Koonce Decl. paras. 2-9.) 2. Summary adjudication is denied as to UCM and Broadmoor as not being proper parties. The moving papers and separate statement do not specifically raise an argument on behalf of Broadmoor. Defendants fails to distinguish between tort and contract causes of action. UCM and Broadmoor can be liable for their tortious conduct. 3. There is a triable issue of material fact as to whether plaintiffs exercised due diligence prior to purchasing/occupying the property. The exposed wires on the pull station, dead bolt locks, and a condominium map that did not refer to the egress all create a triable issue of fact whether Plaintiffs should have discovered the encumbrance. (Koonce Decl. paras. 6-10.)
For all of the reasons set forth above, Defendants have failed to show that Plaintiffs cannot establish the individual causes of action and summary adjudication is denied as to each of them. Further, there is a triable issue of fact as to whether Mr. Alsaidi's knowledge of the pull station and fire exit through Suite 1700 is imputed to defendants. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: LaVenna Ward, CSR # 7077
=(302/MJM)
